channel.title: Whamcloud Community JIRA
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

item.title: [LU-16870] interop sanity test_150b: FAIL: fallocate failed
item.description:
This issue was created by maloo for Minh Diep <mdiep@whamcloud.com>

This issue relates to the following test suite run: https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sets/3e3698b4-6d41-4e16-8288-e104f2582fdf

test_150b failed with the following error:

fallocate failed

Test session details:
clients: https://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-b2_14/2 - 4.18.0-240.22.1.el8_3.x86_64
servers: https://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-b2_15/63 - 4.18.0-477.10.1.el8_lustre.x86_64

<<Please provide additional information about the failure here>>

== sanity test 150b: Verify fallocate (prealloc) functionality ======================================= 06:12:12 (1685167932)
keep default fallocate mode: 0
fallocate failed, error Invalid argument, mode 0, offset 62914560, len 4194304
sanity test_150b: @@@@@@ FAIL: fallocate failed
Trace dump:
= /usr/lib64/lustre/tests/test-framework.sh:6273:error()
= /usr/lib64/lustre/tests/sanity.sh:13441:test_150b()
= /usr/lib64/lustre/tests/test-framework.sh:6576:run_one()
= /usr/lib64/lustre/tests/test-framework.sh:6623:run_one_logged()
= /usr/lib64/lustre/tests/test-framework.sh:6465:run_test()
= /usr/lib64/lustre/tests/sanity.sh:13443:main()

VVVVVVV DO NOT REMOVE LINES BELOW, Added by Maloo for auto-association VVVVVVV
sanity test_150b - fallocate failed
item.summary: interop sanity test_150b: FAIL: fallocate failed

status.description: A resolution has been taken, and it is awaiting verification by reporter. From here issues are either reopened, or are closed.

comments.comment:
"Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>" merged in patch https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/56768/
Subject: LU-16870 tests: Make 413a/b work on server 2.14 with client master
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set:
Commit: 5046ce07c8fdfdaefc03a74be158bb1fdeba25e4
Yes. You are correct and your patch is making it fall back to 'dd' and pass locally. Thanks for the analysis.

The 0x16 opcode is OST_FALLOCATE, and I can confirm that this opcode exists on b2_14.

However, I see in the message that this is MDT0000 so it is trying to send the fallocate to the MDS service? The sanity test_413a/b are doing DoM+fallocate, which wasn't added until commit v2_14_53-10-g163870abfb patch https://review.whamcloud.com/41418 "LU-14382 mdt: implement fallocate in MDC/MDT", so this looks like a straight interop bug.

The check_fallocate_supported() function looks like it is properly being passed mdsN for the facet name, so it should be avoiding the use of fallocate. However, I suspect that the check for fallocate_zero_blocks is always true for osd-ldiskfs, even for MDTs, because the same OSD module is used for OSTs as well. Unfortunately, it looks like there is not an OBD_CONNECT_FALLOCATE flag that could be checked on the client to determine whether the target supports this feature, my bad. However, that is now water under the bridge...

This can just add a version check in check_fallocate_supported() to see if the MDS facet is new enough:

        local fa_mode="osd-ldiskfs.$(facet_svc $facet).fallocate_zero_blocks"
        local mode=$(do_facet $facet $LCTL get_param -n $fa_mode 2>/dev/null |
                     head -n 1)
        [[ "$facet" !~ "mds" ]] || # older MDS doesn't support fallocate
                (( MDS1_VERSION >= $(version_code v2_14_53-10-g163870abfb) )) ||
                mode=""

I don't think this is a real functional regression, since DoM+fallocate didn't work in the past (and in general fallocate() is not guaranteed to work), it is just a test script issue. The test script was changed to use fallocate in commit v2_15_54-53-g09fc9ccb1a ("LU-14824 test: sanity 413a/b unlink timeout v2") so this should be used for the Fixes: line. So in that respect, the test failing interop with b2_14 is a regression in 2.16.
I can recreate this localy. I will update on this.

Looks like it is getting blocked here, with the 2.14 server and master client. I am checking

1905 [  317.912830] LustreError: 16315:0:(tgt_handler.c:633:tgt_handler_find_check()) lustre-MDT0000: unsupported opcode 0x16

I could see this on mds logs

[10803.831795] Lustre: DEBUG MARKER: /usr/sbin/lctl mark == sanity test 413a: QoS mkdir with \'lfs mkdir -i -1\' ==== 21:06:59 \(1729544819\)
[10804.052141] Lustre: DEBUG MARKER: == sanity test 413a: QoS mkdir with 'lfs mkdir -i -1' ==== 21:06:59 (1729544819)
[10804.215822] Lustre: DEBUG MARKER: /usr/sbin/lctl get_param -n lod.lustre-MDT0000-mdtlov.qos_maxage
[10804.514883] Lustre: DEBUG MARKER: /usr/sbin/lctl set_param lod.*.mdt_qos_maxage=1
[10804.881634] Lustre: DEBUG MARKER: /usr/sbin/lctl get_param -n osd-ldiskfs.lustre-MDT0000.fallocate_zero_blocks
[10805.046926] LustreError: 506031:0:(tgt_handler.c:633:tgt_handler_find_check()) lustre-MDT0000: unsupported opcode 0x16
[10805.308211] Lustre: DEBUG MARKER: /usr/sbin/lctl mark  sanity test_413a: @@@@@@ FAIL: fallocate -l 128K  0 failed 
[10805.518336] Lustre: DEBUG MARKER: sanity test_413a: @@@@@@ FAIL: fallocate -l 128K 0 failed

I could not test out yet as I could not setup the interop on my local. It keeps failing for unknown symbols in lfsck.ko, although I am building from source. I did upload the patch to remove the redirection. I keep trying to setup

insmod: ERROR: could not insert module /root/lustre-release/lustre-release/lustre/tests/../lfsck/lfsck.ko: Unknown symbol in module

"Arshad Hussain <arshad.hussain@aeoncomputing.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/56768
Subject: LU-16870 tests: Remove redirect to null from generate_uneven_mdts
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: 13a23251820c2ddbc989bffe0e5305ec331254b6

Thanks. I just did a barrage of fallocate and it worked (on master) hopfully this is the interop as you mentioned.

I cannot seem to access This issue relates to the following test suite run: https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sets/3e3698b4-6d41-4e16-8288-e104f2582fdf   it is giving me permission denied(have done the login). If you can please upload the debug logs i just want to confirm "ERROR" etc in the logs. Rest the local env is getting preped.
The most recent failures were with master clients and b2_14 servers, which is probably more interesting to investigate/fix given the impending 2.16 release.

looking into this. I am setting up the env for local recreation.

clients: https://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-b2_14/2 - 4.18.0-240.22.1.el8_3.x86_64
servers: https://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-b2_15/63 - 4.18.0-477.10.1.el8_lustre.x86_64

arshad512, we are trying to wrap up 2.16 but I notice that there are a number of failures related to fallocate and interop testing between master and b2_14 servers.

This ticket is about test_150b, but fallocate is also being used in test_413a and test_413b and is failing in a similar manner (though it is hard to see why it is failing because the fallocate output is redirected to /dev/null):
https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sets/c2e89b09-33e9-49d2-b82e-61b6bfcbce00

Fill MDT0 with 200 files: loop 0
 sanity test_413a: @@@@@@ FAIL: fallocate -l 128K  0 failed

The check_fallocate_supported() function is passing with b2_14 servers, and "fallocate_zero_blocks that is being checked by this test was added in commit v2_13_57-67-g4f18e08099 patch https://review.whamcloud.com/41204 "LU-14286 osd-ldiskfs: fallocate with unwritten extents" so definitely some core fallocate support was available in 2.14.0.

Could you please take a look into this, since I recall you did a bunch of work in this area already and could probably come up with the answer quickly? At a minimum to determine if this is some new issue with master, or if it is some old interop issue that could be ignored by changing check_fallocate_supported() to be more selective.

If you push a patch, please remove the /dev/null redirection from generate_uneven_mdts() since that was mostly needed only for "dd" and we will rarely (if ever) be needing that again (2.12 interop is going away), and fallocate does not generate any output upon successful completion.

Thanks.
